page: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning workloads. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8570 processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it integrates two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with a combined total of 384 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, meaning external storage solutions must be attached.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 97,240.84 and a PassMark CPU score of 88,533.65. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 7,105.96 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 385.14 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are enhanced by hardware flags including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions). Single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks also place in the top tier, with gzip compression reaching 34.78 GB/sec.

The inclusion of dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs makes this server highly efficient for artificial intelligence workloads. Prompt processing for a small 135M parameter LLM model achieves a top-tier speed of 9,458.91 tokens per second, while text generation reaches 317.76 tokens per second. Database operations are also highly performant, with Redis SET operations executing at 117.34 million operations per second. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated core instances. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, deep learning training, data science pipelines, and high-throughput database operations.
description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a high-performance GPU-accelerated server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8570 processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, paired with 480.0 GB of system memory. The server's primary strength lies in its dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, which deliver a total of 384 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth, strong multi-core processing, and exceptional database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 117.34 million operations per second. While local storage is absent, the massive GPU memory and system RAM make this instance highly cost-efficient for resource-dense AI workloads. It is optimized for large language model inference, deep learning, and complex data science tasks.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 Intel Xeon cores, 480 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 384 GB VRAM for demanding AI and deep learning workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 features 48 Intel Xeon cores, 480 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs for high-performance AI workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring dual NVIDIA Blackwell accelerators and massive system memory for advanced artificial intelligence and deep learning workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 384 GB total VRAM
48 Intel Xeon 8570 physical cores with 480 GB system memory
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7,105.96 GB/sec
Exceptional LLM prompt processing and Redis database throughput
Best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and data science
